title: orchestration run journal entry (envelope + per-command payload)
description: One append-only entry in a per-run journal (portfolio/logs/<run>.jsonl). One entry PER COMMAND (a host-fired hook command or an agent-called orchestration command) — never one per step: a step is reconstructed from the command entries its session brackets. A shared ENVELOPE ties every entry to its run + step so the journal replays the workflow (grouping entries by `step` reconstructs each step; the ordered `dispatch` entries are the run's step sequence). A typed PAYLOAD (discriminated union, oneOf on `command`) carries that command's output: a hook command's payload IS its decision/report; an orchestration command's payload IS its action. The journal is the audit + trace, NOT the branching input — sequencing recomputes from artifacts, never from a prior entry. See README invariant 12 and the Logging section.

command.description: The harness command that produced this entry (the granularity of the journal).

trigger.description: What fired the command: an agent-called orchestration command, or a host-fired hook command.

run.description: Run id selecting the per-run journal. May be null on a session-scoped entry not yet correlated to a run (the session→run binding is established by dispatch correlation).

session.description: Host session id of the entry's session (one session = one step). The dispatched step sessions correlate to the run via this id.

orchestration.description: Contract id of the orchestration the step belongs to (root orchestration or suborchestration).

step.description: Structurant step id, matching one steps[].id in the orchestration contract. Grouping entries by `step` reconstructs each step.

unit.description: Epic / Feature / Story id the step acts on.

actor.description: Agent that executed the command (matched against acl.yaml for write-authority).

ts.description: Optional ISO-8601 timestamp; JSONL file order remains the authoritative ordering.

status.description: Outcome at append time: a step outcome (started/completed/blocked/failed), an action verb (dispatch/halt/done/error), or a hook permission (allow/deny), or observed for a no-enforcement phase.

payload.description: The command's typed output (see the per-command payload schemas selected by the oneOf below).

oneOf.description: hook: the host decision the harness returned.
check-artifact carries a free report payload.
